title: Trigonostemon semperflorens
taxonomy: Trigonostemon semperflorens belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the spurge family. This family consists of a wide variety of flowering plants, including trees, shrubs, and herbs. Trigonostemon semperflorens is native to tropical regions of Asia, where it thrives in warm and humid climates. The plant is part of the genus Trigonostemon, which comprises approximately 12 species of flowering plants. Further classification of Trigonostemon semperflorens includes its order (Malpighiales), class (Magnoliopsida), and division (Angiospermae).

intro: Trigonostemon semperflorens, also known as Everblooming Tornado Flower, is a perennial plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Trigonostemon semperflorens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly prepared as an herbal infusion, tincture, or decoction, and is used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
